question: According to Table 1.3-1, how does NuScale's design life compare to a typical PWR?

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, and safety system components, showing that NuScale uses smaller, simpler modules with fewer external systems and a longer design life. The comparison is summarized in tables for easy reference.

nuscale_ch01_0050.title: Diesel Generator Buildings
nuscale_ch01_0050.summary: The Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs) in the NuScale Power Plant each house a single backup diesel generator and their auxiliary equipment, but do not contain any safety-related systems or support engineered safety functions (ESFs). Their primary purpose is to provide support and housing for the generators, associated equipment, HVAC system, and maintenance area.

nuscale_ch01_0049.title: Central Utility Building
nuscale_ch01_0049.summary: The Central Utility Building (CUB) is a nonsafety-related structure that houses essential utility services for the plant, such as chiller equipment, air systems, demineralized water equipment, and security functions. The design also includes Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs) for backup power, and emphasizes human factors engineering to minimize human error and optimize control room operations through automation and digital technologies.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4. It details whether each regulatory guide is applicable, and if so, whether NuScale’s design fully or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections for further information. Several guides are noted as not applicable, while others are partially or fully met.
